# TanStack Query (React Query) v5

TanStack Query is an async state management library for web applications, purpose-built to simplify fetching, caching, synchronizing, and updating server state. Unlike traditional client-side state managers, it treats server data as a first-class citizen with a dedicated lifecycle: queries are uniquely keyed, automatically cached, background-refetched when stale, and garbage-collected when unused. It is protocol-agnostic — it works with REST, GraphQL, WebSockets, or any Promise-returning function — and supports React, Vue, Solid, Svelte, Angular, Lit, and Preact through separate adapter packages (`@tanstack/react-query`, `@tanstack/vue-query`, etc.), all sharing the same `@tanstack/query-core` engine.

The core functionality revolves around three building blocks: **queries** (declarative data reads via `useQuery`), **mutations** (imperative data writes via `useMutation`), and the **QueryClient** (the cache controller). Queries are identified by serializable query keys and automatically re-execute when those keys change. Mutations can trigger cache invalidations, optimistic updates, and offline persistence. The library ships with React Suspense support (`useSuspenseQuery`), infinite/paginated query helpers (`useInfiniteQuery`), parallel/dynamic query execution (`useQueries`), server-side rendering hydration utilities (`dehydrate`/`hydrate`), and an ESLint plugin for enforcing correct patterns. Current stable version is **v5** (`@tanstack/react-query@5.x`).

---

## Installation and Setup

Install the library and wrap the application in `QueryClientProvider` with a `QueryClient` instance.

```tsx
npm install @tanstack/react-query
# Optional devtools
npm install @tanstack/react-query-devtools

import {
  QueryClient,
  QueryClientProvider,
} from '@tanstack/react-query'
import { ReactQueryDevtools } from '@tanstack/react-query-devtools'

// Create once per app lifecycle (or once per request in SSR — see SSR section)
const queryClient = new QueryClient({
  defaultOptions: {
    queries: {
      staleTime: 60 * 1000,      // data is fresh for 1 min
      gcTime: 5 * 60 * 1000,     // unused cache kept for 5 min
      retry: 3,                  // retry failed queries 3 times
      refetchOnWindowFocus: true,
    },
  },
})

export default function App() {
  return (
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
      <YourApp />
      <ReactQueryDevtools initialIsOpen={false} />
    </QueryClientProvider>
  )
}
```

---

## `useQuery` — Fetch and Cache Data

Subscribes a component to an async data source. Returns live-updating `data`, `status`, `error`, and fetch state flags. Automatically refetches when the query key changes.

```tsx
import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'

// Basic fetch with full status handling
function UserProfile({ userId }: { userId: number }) {
  const { data, isPending, isError, error, isFetching, refetch } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['user', userId],          // unique cache key — reruns on userId change
    queryFn: async () => {
      const res = await fetch(`/api/users/${userId}`)
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Network response was not ok')
      return res.json() as Promise<{ id: number; name: string; email: string }>
    },
    staleTime: 30_000,                   // treat data as fresh for 30s
    gcTime: 5 * 60_000,                  // keep unused data in cache for 5 min
    refetchOnWindowFocus: true,
    retry: (failureCount, error) => failureCount < 2,
    select: (data) => ({ ...data, name: data.name.toUpperCase() }), // transform data
  })

  if (isPending) return <p>Loading...</p>
  if (isError)   return <p>Error: {error.message}</p>

  return (
    <div>
      <h1>{data.name}</h1>
      <p>{data.email}</p>
      {isFetching && <span>(refreshing…)</span>}
      <button onClick={() => refetch()}>Refresh</button>
    </div>
  )
}
```

---

## Query Keys — Unique Cache Identifiers

Query keys are serializable arrays that uniquely identify cached data. All variables used inside `queryFn` must appear in the key so the cache invalidates correctly when they change.

```tsx
import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'

// Static key — shared across the whole app
useQuery({ queryKey: ['todos'], queryFn: fetchTodos })

// Key with ID — each ID gets its own cache entry
useQuery({ queryKey: ['todo', 5], queryFn: () => fetchTodoById(5) })

// Key with filter object — object key order does NOT matter (hashed deterministically)
function TodoList({ status, page }: { status: string; page: number }) {
  return useQuery({
    queryKey: ['todos', { status, page }],   // re-fetches whenever status or page changes
    queryFn: ({ queryKey }) => {
      const [, { status, page }] = queryKey  // key is passed into queryFn context
      return fetchTodos({ status, page })
    },
  })
}

// Keys are also used with the QueryClient for targeted invalidation:
// queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })  ← invalidates all todos/*
// queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todo', 5], exact: true }) ← exact match
```

---

## `queryOptions` — Reusable Query Definitions

Co-locate `queryKey`, `queryFn`, and options in one place for full type-safety and reuse across `useQuery`, `prefetchQuery`, `setQueryData`, and more.

```tsx
import { queryOptions, useQuery, useSuspenseQuery, useQueries } from '@tanstack/react-query'

// Define once
function groupOptions(id: number) {
  return queryOptions({
    queryKey: ['groups', id],
    queryFn: (): Promise<{ id: number; name: string; members: string[] }> =>
      fetch(`/api/groups/${id}`).then((r) => r.json()),
    staleTime: 5_000,
  })
}

// Use anywhere with full TypeScript inference
function GroupDetail({ id }: { id: number }) {
  const { data } = useQuery(groupOptions(id))
  // data is typed as { id: number; name: string; members: string[] }
  return <h2>{data?.name}</h2>
}

// Reuse in parallel queries
function MultiGroup({ ids }: { ids: number[] }) {
  const results = useQueries({ queries: ids.map(groupOptions) })
  return <ul>{results.map((r) => <li key={r.data?.id}>{r.data?.name}</li>)}</ul>
}

// Reuse in prefetch (e.g., in a route loader)
await queryClient.prefetchQuery(groupOptions(42))

// Reuse in setQueryData with correct key type
queryClient.setQueryData(groupOptions(42).queryKey, { id: 42, name: 'Admins', members: [] })

// Override options per component
const { data: nameOnly } = useQuery({ ...groupOptions(1), select: (d) => d.name })
```

---

## Parallel Queries with `useQueries`

Execute a dynamic number of queries in parallel. Returns an array of query result objects.

```tsx
import { useQuery, useQueries } from '@tanstack/react-query'

// Static parallel — just call multiple useQuery hooks
function Dashboard() {
  const usersQuery    = useQuery({ queryKey: ['users'],    queryFn: fetchUsers })
  const teamsQuery    = useQuery({ queryKey: ['teams'],    queryFn: fetchTeams })
  const projectsQuery = useQuery({ queryKey: ['projects'], queryFn: fetchProjects })
  // All three fetch simultaneously
  return <div>{/* render results */}</div>
}

// Dynamic parallel — number of queries is unknown at render time
function UserMessages({ userIds }: { userIds: number[] }) {
  const messageQueries = useQueries({
    queries: userIds.map((id) => ({
      queryKey: ['messages', id] as const,
      queryFn: () => fetchMessagesByUser(id),
      staleTime: 10_000,
    })),
    combine: (results) => ({        // optional: combine all results into one value
      data: results.map((r) => r.data),
      isPending: results.some((r) => r.isPending),
    }),
  })

  if (messageQueries.isPending) return <p>Loading…</p>
  return (
    <ul>
      {messageQueries.data.map((msgs, i) => (
        <li key={userIds[i]}>{msgs?.length} messages</li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  )
}
```

---

## Dependent (Serial) Queries

Chain queries that depend on the result of a prior fetch by using the `enabled` flag.

```tsx
import { useQuery, useQueries } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function UserProjects({ email }: { email: string }) {
  // Step 1: fetch user
  const { data: user } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['user', email],
    queryFn: () => fetchUserByEmail(email),
  })

  // Step 2: fetch projects only when userId is known
  const { data: projects, status, fetchStatus } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['projects', user?.id],
    queryFn: () => fetchProjectsByUser(user!.id),
    enabled: !!user?.id,   // query is paused until user.id exists
  })

  // status === 'pending' + fetchStatus === 'idle'  → waiting for user
  // status === 'pending' + fetchStatus === 'fetching' → actively loading
  // status === 'success' → data ready

  return (
    <div>
      {user && <h2>Projects for {user.name}</h2>}
      {fetchStatus === 'fetching' && <p>Loading projects…</p>}
      <ul>{projects?.map((p) => <li key={p.id}>{p.name}</li>)}</ul>
    </div>
  )
}
```

---

## `useMutation` — Create, Update, Delete

Imperatively triggers async operations with full lifecycle callbacks. Supports optimistic updates, retries, and serialized mutation scopes.

```tsx
import { useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

interface Todo { id: number; title: string; done: boolean }

function AddTodo() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  const mutation = useMutation({
    mutationFn: (newTodo: { title: string }) =>
      fetch('/api/todos', {
        method: 'POST',
        body: JSON.stringify(newTodo),
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      }).then((r) => r.json() as Promise<Todo>),

    onMutate: async (newTodo, context) => {
      await context.client.cancelQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })       // cancel in-flight refetches
      const previous = context.client.getQueryData<Todo[]>(['todos'])   // snapshot
      context.client.setQueryData<Todo[]>(['todos'], (old = []) => [    // optimistic update
        ...old,
        { id: Date.now(), title: newTodo.title, done: false },
      ])
      return { previous }                                                // returned as onMutateResult
    },

    onError: (_err, _vars, onMutateResult, context) => {
      // roll back on failure
      context.client.setQueryData(['todos'], onMutateResult?.previous)
    },

    onSettled: (_data, _error, _vars, _onMutateResult, context) => {
      context.client.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })         // sync with server
    },
  })

  const handleSubmit = (e: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) => {
    e.preventDefault()
    const form = e.currentTarget
    const title = (form.elements.namedItem('title') as HTMLInputElement).value
    mutation.mutate({ title })
    form.reset()
  }

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
      {mutation.isError  && <p style={{ color: 'red' }}>{mutation.error.message}</p>}
      {mutation.isSuccess && <p>Todo added! ID: {mutation.data.id}</p>}
      <input name="title" required />
      <button type="submit" disabled={mutation.isPending}>
        {mutation.isPending ? 'Adding…' : 'Add Todo'}
      </button>
    </form>
  )
}
```

---

## `useInfiniteQuery` — Paginated & Infinite Scroll

Fetches paginated data with built-in support for cursor-based, offset-based, and bi-directional pagination.

```tsx
import { useInfiniteQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'

interface Page { data: Array<{ id: number; name: string }>; nextCursor?: number }

function ProjectList() {
  const {
    data,
    fetchNextPage,
    fetchPreviousPage,
    hasNextPage,
    hasPreviousPage,
    isFetchingNextPage,
    isFetching,
    status,
    error,
  } = useInfiniteQuery({
    queryKey: ['projects'],
    queryFn: async ({ pageParam }): Promise<Page> => {
      const res = await fetch(`/api/projects?cursor=${pageParam}&limit=10`)
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Failed to fetch')
      return res.json()
    },
    initialPageParam: 0,                                        // required in v5
    getNextPageParam: (lastPage) => lastPage.nextCursor,        // undefined = no more pages
    getPreviousPageParam: (firstPage) => firstPage.prevCursor,  // for bi-directional lists
    maxPages: 5,                                                // cap memory: keep only 5 pages
  })

  if (status === 'pending') return <p>Loading…</p>
  if (status === 'error')   return <p>Error: {(error as Error).message}</p>

  return (
    <>
      {data.pages.map((page, i) => (
        <React.Fragment key={i}>
          {page.data.map((project) => (
            <div key={project.id}>{project.name}</div>
          ))}
        </React.Fragment>
      ))}

      <button
        onClick={() => fetchNextPage()}
        disabled={!hasNextPage || isFetchingNextPage}
      >
        {isFetchingNextPage ? 'Loading more…' : hasNextPage ? 'Load More' : 'End of list'}
      </button>

      {isFetching && !isFetchingNextPage && <span>Background refresh…</span>}
    </>
  )
}
```

---

## `useSuspenseQuery` — React Suspense Integration

Uses React's Suspense boundary for loading states and error boundaries for errors. `data` is always defined (never `undefined`) within the component body.

```tsx
import {
  useSuspenseQuery,
  useSuspenseQueries,
  usePrefetchQuery,
  QueryErrorResetBoundary,
} from '@tanstack/react-query'
import { Suspense } from 'react'
import { ErrorBoundary } from 'react-error-boundary'

// Parent: start prefetching before Suspense renders
function ArticleLayout({ id }: { id: number }) {
  usePrefetchQuery({
    queryKey: ['comments', id],
    queryFn: () => fetchComments(id),
  })

  return (
    <QueryErrorResetBoundary>
      {({ reset }) => (
        <ErrorBoundary onReset={reset} fallbackRender={({ resetErrorBoundary }) =>
          <button onClick={resetErrorBoundary}>Retry</button>
        }>
          <Suspense fallback={<p>Loading article…</p>}>
            <Article id={id} />
          </Suspense>
        </ErrorBoundary>
      )}
    </QueryErrorResetBoundary>
  )
}

// Child: data is guaranteed non-undefined; no isPending/isError needed
function Article({ id }: { id: number }) {
  // Both queries run in PARALLEL with useSuspenseQueries
  const [{ data: article }, { data: comments }] = useSuspenseQueries({
    queries: [
      { queryKey: ['article', id],  queryFn: () => fetchArticle(id) },
      { queryKey: ['comments', id], queryFn: () => fetchComments(id) },
    ],
  })

  return (
    <div>
      <h1>{article.title}</h1>
      <p>{article.body}</p>
      <h2>Comments ({comments.length})</h2>
      {comments.map((c) => <p key={c.id}>{c.text}</p>)}
    </div>
  )
}
```

---

## `QueryClient` — Cache Controller

The central object for interacting with the cache imperatively: prefetching, manual data writes, invalidation, and cancellation.

```tsx
import { QueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

const queryClient = new QueryClient({
  defaultOptions: { queries: { staleTime: Infinity } },
})

// --- Prefetch before render ---
await queryClient.prefetchQuery({ queryKey: ['posts'], queryFn: fetchPosts })

// --- Fetch and get data (throws on error) ---
const posts = await queryClient.fetchQuery({
  queryKey: ['posts'],
  queryFn: fetchPosts,
  staleTime: 10_000,   // only re-fetch if older than 10s
})

// --- Read from cache synchronously ---
const cached = queryClient.getQueryData<Post[]>(['posts'])

// --- Write to cache synchronously (triggers re-renders) ---
queryClient.setQueryData<Post[]>(['posts'], (old = []) => [
  ...old,
  { id: 99, title: 'New Post' },
])

// --- Invalidate (mark stale + refetch active queries) ---
await queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['posts'] })
// Only mark stale, do not refetch:
await queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['posts'], refetchType: 'none' })
// Refetch active AND inactive:
await queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['posts'], refetchType: 'all' })

// --- Cancel in-flight queries (used before optimistic updates) ---
await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'], exact: true })

// --- Remove queries entirely from cache ---
queryClient.removeQueries({ queryKey: ['stale-data'], exact: true })

// --- Reset to initial data state ---
await queryClient.resetQueries({ queryKey: ['posts'] })

// --- Set per-key defaults (useful for code-splitting) ---
queryClient.setQueryDefaults(['posts'], { queryFn: fetchPosts, staleTime: 30_000 })
queryClient.setMutationDefaults(['addPost'], { mutationFn: addPost })

// --- Global loading indicator ---
console.log(queryClient.isFetching())   // number of in-flight queries
console.log(queryClient.isMutating())   // number of in-flight mutations

// --- Clear entire cache ---
queryClient.clear()
```

---

## Optimistic Updates

Update the UI before the server confirms, with automatic rollback on failure.

```tsx
import { useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

interface Todo { id: number; title: string; done: boolean }

// --- Pattern 1: Via the cache (supports multiple components showing the update) ---
function useUpdateTodo() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  return useMutation({
    mutationFn: (todo: Todo) =>
      fetch(`/api/todos/${todo.id}`, {
        method: 'PATCH',
        body: JSON.stringify(todo),
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      }).then((r) => r.json()),

    onMutate: async (newTodo, context) => {
      await context.client.cancelQueries({ queryKey: ['todos', newTodo.id] })
      const previousTodo = context.client.getQueryData<Todo>(['todos', newTodo.id])
      context.client.setQueryData<Todo>(['todos', newTodo.id], newTodo)
      return { previousTodo }
    },
    onError: (_err, newTodo, onMutateResult, context) => {
      context.client.setQueryData(
        ['todos', newTodo.id],
        onMutateResult?.previousTodo,
      )
    },
    onSettled: (_data, _err, newTodo, _ctx, context) => {
      context.client.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos', newTodo.id] })
    },
  })
}

// --- Pattern 2: Via mutation variables (simpler, single-component) ---
function TodoItem({ todo }: { todo: Todo }) {
  const { mutate, isPending, variables, isError } = useMutation({
    mutationFn: (updated: Todo) => patchTodo(updated),
    onSettled: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] }),
  })

  // Show optimistic title immediately while mutation is pending
  const displayTitle = isPending ? variables?.title : todo.title

  return (
    <li style={{ opacity: isPending ? 0.5 : 1 }}>
      {displayTitle}
      {isError && <button onClick={() => mutate({ ...todo, title: variables!.title })}>Retry</button>}
      <button onClick={() => mutate({ ...todo, title: 'Updated!' })}>Update</button>
    </li>
  )
}
```

---

## Prefetching Strategies

Warm the cache before data is needed to eliminate loading states.

```tsx
import { useQueryClient, usePrefetchQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'
import { useEffect } from 'react'

const todoOptions = (id: number) => ({
  queryKey: ['todo', id] as const,
  queryFn: () => fetchTodoById(id),
  staleTime: 60_000,
})

// 1. On user interaction (hover / focus)
function TodoLink({ id }: { id: number }) {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
  return (
    <a
      href={`/todos/${id}`}
      onMouseEnter={() => queryClient.prefetchQuery(todoOptions(id))}
      onFocus={() => queryClient.prefetchQuery(todoOptions(id))}
    >
      View Todo
    </a>
  )
}

// 2. In a parent component (parallel to primary query, avoids waterfall)
function Article({ id }: { id: number }) {
  usePrefetchQuery({ queryKey: ['article-comments', id], queryFn: () => fetchComments(id) })

  const { data } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['article', id], queryFn: () => fetchArticle(id) })
  return <div>{data?.title}</div>
}

// 3. Inside queryFn (when secondary data is always needed alongside primary)
function Feed() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
  const { data } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['feed'],
    queryFn: async (...args) => {
      const feed = await getFeed(...args)
      feed
        .filter((item) => item.type === 'GRAPH')
        .forEach((item) =>
          queryClient.prefetchQuery({ queryKey: ['graph', item.id], queryFn: () => getGraphData(item.id) })
        )
      return feed
    },
  })
  return <div>{/* render feed */}</div>
}

// 4. Manually prime the cache with synchronously available data
queryClient.setQueryData(['todos'], existingTodosFromProps)
```

---

## Server-Side Rendering (SSR) and Hydration

Prefetch data on the server, dehydrate it into the HTML payload, and rehydrate on the client to avoid a second network round-trip.

```tsx
// ─── Next.js Pages Router ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// pages/_app.tsx
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from '@tanstack/react-query'
import { useState } from 'react'

export default function MyApp({ Component, pageProps }) {
  // MUST be in state so each request gets its own cache (no cross-user data leaks)
  const [queryClient] = useState(() => new QueryClient({
    defaultOptions: { queries: { staleTime: 60_000 } },
  }))

  return (
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
      <Component {...pageProps} />
    </QueryClientProvider>
  )
}

// pages/posts.tsx
import { dehydrate, HydrationBoundary, QueryClient, useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'

export async function getServerSideProps() {
  const queryClient = new QueryClient()
  await queryClient.prefetchQuery({ queryKey: ['posts'], queryFn: fetchPosts })

  return {
    props: {
      dehydratedState: dehydrate(queryClient),   // serialized cache → HTML
    },
  }
}

export default function PostsPage({ dehydratedState }) {
  return (
    <HydrationBoundary state={dehydratedState}>  {/* rehydrate on client */}
      <Posts />
    </HydrationBoundary>
  )
}

function Posts() {
  // Instantly returns server-prefetched data; no loading state on first render
  const { data } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['posts'], queryFn: fetchPosts })
  return <ul>{data?.map((p) => <li key={p.id}>{p.title}</li>)}</ul>
}
```

---

## `useIsFetching` and `useIsMutating` — Global Loading Indicators

Subscribe to the count of in-flight queries or mutations across the entire application.

```tsx
import {
  useIsFetching,
  useIsMutating,
  useMutationState,
} from '@tanstack/react-query'

// Global spinner — shown whenever any query is fetching
function GlobalSpinner() {
  const isFetching = useIsFetching()
  return isFetching ? <div className="spinner" /> : null
}

// Scoped to a specific query key prefix
function TodosLoadingBar() {
  const fetchingTodos = useIsFetching({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
  return fetchingTodos > 0 ? <progress /> : null
}

// Disable save button while any mutation is running
function SaveButton() {
  const isMutating = useIsMutating()
  return <button disabled={isMutating > 0}>Save</button>
}

// Access pending mutation variables from anywhere (cross-component optimistic updates)
function OptimisticTodoList() {
  const pendingNewTodos = useMutationState<string>({
    filters: { mutationKey: ['addTodo'], status: 'pending' },
    select: (mutation) => mutation.state.variables as string,
  })

  return (
    <ul>
      {/* real todos from useQuery … */}
      {pendingNewTodos.map((title, i) => (
        <li key={i} style={{ opacity: 0.5 }}>{title} (saving…)</li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  )
}
```

---

## ESLint Plugin `@tanstack/eslint-plugin-query`

Enforce best practices at lint time: exhaustive deps in query keys, stable query client references, and proper mutation patterns.

```js
// eslint.config.js (flat config)
import pluginQuery from '@tanstack/eslint-plugin-query'

export default [
  ...pluginQuery.configs['flat/recommended'],
  // Recommended enables:
  // - @tanstack/query/exhaustive-deps           (queryKey must include all deps used in queryFn)
  // - @tanstack/query/no-unstable-deps          (no inline objects/functions as deps)
  // - @tanstack/query/stable-query-client       (QueryClient must not be created inside render)
  // - @tanstack/query/no-rest-destructuring     (no ...rest from useQuery — use specific fields)
  // - @tanstack/query/infinite-query-property-order
  // - @tanstack/query/mutation-property-order
]

// Example violation caught by exhaustive-deps:
function Bad({ userId }) {
  useQuery({
    queryKey: ['user'],        // ❌ userId is used in queryFn but missing from key
    queryFn: () => fetchUser(userId),
  })
}

// Fixed:
function Good({ userId }) {
  useQuery({
    queryKey: ['user', userId], // ✅
    queryFn: () => fetchUser(userId),
  })
}
```

---

## Mutation Persistence and Offline Support

Persist pending mutations across page reloads and resume them when the device reconnects.

```tsx
import {
  QueryClient,
  useMutation,
} from '@tanstack/react-query'
import {
  PersistQueryClientProvider,
  createSyncStoragePersister,
} from '@tanstack/react-query-persist-client'
import { dehydrate, hydrate } from '@tanstack/react-query'

const persister = createSyncStoragePersister({ storage: window.localStorage })

const queryClient = new QueryClient({
  defaultOptions: { queries: { gcTime: 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24 } }, // 24h
})

// Register default mutation fn so paused mutations can resume after reload
queryClient.setMutationDefaults(['todos'], {
  mutationFn: ({ id, data }: { id: number; data: Partial<Todo> }) =>
    fetch(`/api/todos/${id}`, { method: 'PATCH', body: JSON.stringify(data) }).then((r) => r.json()),
})

export default function App() {
  return (
    <PersistQueryClientProvider
      client={queryClient}
      persistOptions={{ persister }}
      onSuccess={() => {
        // After cache is restored from localStorage, resume paused (offline) mutations
        queryClient.resumePausedMutations()
          .then(() => queryClient.invalidateQueries())
      }}
    >
      <TodoApp />
    </PersistQueryClientProvider>
  )
}

function TodoApp() {
  const mutation = useMutation({
    mutationKey: ['todos'],    // links to the default set above
    scope: { id: 'todo' },     // serialize mutations with same scope.id (no parallel writes)
    retry: 3,
  })

  return (
    <button onClick={() => mutation.mutate({ id: 1, data: { done: true } })}>
      {mutation.isPending ? 'Saving…' : 'Mark Done'}
    </button>
  )
}
```

---

TanStack Query excels in two primary use cases: **data-heavy client applications** where server state needs to stay fresh and in sync (dashboards, admin panels, social feeds, e-commerce), and **full-stack SSR/SSG applications** where eliminating client-side loading waterfalls is critical for performance (Next.js, Remix, SolidStart). Its stale-while-revalidate cache strategy means users always see data immediately while fresh data loads in the background. The query key system enables surgical cache invalidation — after a mutation you can invalidate exactly the affected queries, or even optimistically update the cache before the server responds. Combined with the `queryOptions` factory pattern, teams can centralize all server interaction in domain-specific modules that are consumed uniformly by hooks, prefetch calls, and imperative `QueryClient` operations.

Integration patterns converge on a few standard recipes: (1) **router-level prefetching** — populate the cache in route loaders before the component tree renders, (2) **SSR hydration** — dehydrate the QueryClient on the server and rehydrate inside `<HydrationBoundary>` on the client, (3) **mutation-driven invalidation** — call `invalidateQueries` in `onSettled` to keep the cache consistent after any write, and (4) **optimistic UI** — use `onMutate`/`onError` with `setQueryData`/rollback for instant feedback. The library is framework-adaptor-based, so the same patterns apply to Vue (`useQuery` from `@tanstack/vue-query`), Svelte, Angular (`injectQuery` from `@tanstack/angular-query-experimental`), and others, each using framework-idiomatic reactivity primitives while sharing the same `@tanstack/query-core` cache engine.
